FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class SpanRunTable

Namespace
FluentGpu.Foundation
Assembly
FluentGpu.Foundation.dll

Process-wide registry of span runs — the interner-style side channel that lets the multi-run style overlay ride an int through the POD pipeline (TextStyle.SpanRunId → DrawList DrawGlyphRunCmd.SpanRunId) the way strings ride StringId through StringTable.

Seam-safe (threading-render-seam.md §9, the StringTable discipline): chunked backing store — chunks are allocated once and never moved; a release-store of the count publishes each new slot, the reader acquire-loads the count before indexing. The UI thread is the single writer (reconciler, at element-change time — never in frame phases 6–13); the render-side glyph path resolves the previous frame's ids concurrently.

Static Shared (deliberate): unlike StringTable (host-instanced and threaded through every constructor), the render-side consumer reaches a span run only through the POD id baked in the draw stream — there is no host seam to hand an instance through. One process = one UI thread = one writer, ids burn monotonically and are never reused, so a process-global table is safe by the same argument that makes the ids safe.

Reclamation: owners (the scene's span-text side-table) pair AddRef(int)/Release(int); a run whose count hits 0 has its slot cleared after a creation-distance quarantine (≥ FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanRunTable.QuarantineDistance newer runs registered — far beyond the in-flight frame depth at any realistic churn), so a draw list recorded just before the release still resolves. A cleared id resolves null → consumers fall back to the base style. Ids are never reused.

public sealed class SpanRunTable

public static readonly SpanRunTable Shared

Field Value

SpanRunTable

Methods

AddRef(int)

Take an ownership reference (UI thread; the scene's span-text side-table is the owner).

public void AddRef(int id)

Parameters

id int

Create(SpanStyle[])

Register a span run (UI thread). The spans array is taken by reference and must not be mutated after.

public int Create(SpanStyle[] spans)

Parameters

spans SpanStyle[]

Returns

int

Release(int)

Drop an ownership reference (UI thread). The last release schedules the slot clear behind the creation-distance quarantine. Releasing id 0 / an unknown id is a no-op.

public void Release(int id)

Parameters

id int

Resolve(int)

Resolve an id to its run, or null (id 0 / reclaimed). Safe from the render thread concurrently with Create(SpanStyle[]) (acquire on count, chunks never move).

public SpanRun? Resolve(int id)

Parameters

id int

Returns

SpanRun
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
